title: JSON Formatter & Validator — Format, Minify, and Validate
description: Why do 90% of developers waste time on messy JSON? Instantly format, minify, and fix your code online.

schemas.name: JSON Formatter & Validator
schemas.description: Interactive tool to format, validate, minify, and sort JSON. Shows syntax errors with line numbers and provides syntax-highlighted output.

acceptedAnswer.text: A JSON formatter is a tool that takes raw, minified, or unformatted JSON code and formats it by adding proper indentation, line breaks, and spaces, making it readable for humans and easier to debug.

mainEntity.name: How does a JSON validator online work?

acceptedAnswer.text: An online JSON validator parses your JSON string to check if it conforms to strict JSON syntax rules. If it finds errors like missing brackets, single quotes, or trailing commas, it will alert you to the exact line and column where the error occurred.

mainEntity.name: How can I JSON pretty print my code?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can use an online formatter to 'pretty print' your JSON. Simply paste your unformatted JSON into the input field, and the tool will instantly add indentation (typically spaces) and syntax highlighting.

mainEntity.name: Why is it important to format JSON?

acceptedAnswer.text: Formatting JSON is crucial for readability and debugging. While APIs and servers often send minified JSON to save bandwidth, formatted JSON allows developers to quickly grasp the nested structure of data and spot syntax errors.

mainEntity.name: Are there tools to automatically fix invalid JSON?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, many modern online JSON formatters have built-in fixers. They can automatically detect and resolve common mistakes like trailing commas, single quotes instead of double quotes, and unquoted keys.

schemas.headline: JSON Formatter & Validator: Pretty Print, Minify & Fix JSON Online
schemas.description: Interactive tool to format, validate, minify, and sort JSON. Shows syntax errors with line numbers and provides syntax-highlighted output.
